issues.title: First user & ticket
issues.body:
Its a nice project using GSD, so I just wanted to try it out and play a bit.

Too bad gives a "Could not resolve host":

```
curl -sSf https://mesh-lang.org/install.sh | sh
```

Doesn't solve for me. I also tried `curl -sSf https://meshlang.dev/install.sh| sh` (gives a 404)

issues.title: Installer succeeds but CLI not available (PATH not set / session not updated)
issues.body:
After installing Mesh using the official installers, the CLI tools (meshc, meshpkg) are not immediately available in the terminal, even though the installer reports success.

This creates a confusing onboarding experience where installation appears successful, but the commands fail with command not found.

issues.title: meshc only detects runtime in hardcoded paths (~/.mesh/target/{release,debug}) causing build failure after install
issues.body: After installing Mesh and attempting to build a simple project, the build fails because the compiler cannot locate the required runtime library (libmesh_rt.a), even when it exists in expected or intuitive locations.

issues.title: Windows: npm run generate:og fails in website
issues.body:
Running `npm run generate:og` in the website project fails on Windows because the script uses `python3`, which is not available by default on many Windows systems.

---

### Affected File
- `website/package.json`

issues.title: [Bug]: website Packages nav link points to /packages instead of opening packages.meshlang.dev in a new tab
issues.body:
## Before you submit
- [x] I searched existing issues and checked the relevant docs/examples first.
- [x] I can reproduce this with a current build, the latest public release, or a specific commit I list below.

## Affected area
documentation / website

## Version / commit
79a030c8

## Operating system
Other

## Project / starter / path (if relevant)
- `website/docs/.vitepress/config.mts`
- `website/docs/.vitepress/theme/components/NavBar.vue`

## Reproduction steps
1. Open the website.
2. Click the **Packages** link in the top navigation bar.
3. Observe that it navigates the current tab to `/packages`.

## Expected behavior
The **Packages** nav item should open `https://packages.meshlang.dev` in a new browser tab.

## Actual behavior
The nav item points at the local site route (`/packages` / `/packages/`) instead of the external packages site, so it keeps the user on the docs site and sends them to the wrong destination.

## Logs, errors, or screenshots
Relevant repo surfaces currently point to the local path:
- `website/docs/.vitepress/config.mts:218` → `{ text: 'Packages', link: '/packages/' }`
- `website/docs/.vitepress/theme/components/NavBar.vue:17` → `{ text: 'Packages', href: '/packages/' }`

## Additional context
This appears to affect the shared website navigation surface rather than page-local content. The desired behavior is an external link to `packages.meshlang.dev`, opened in a new tab/window.

issues.title: Hyperpush: JS SDK true drop-in Sentry compatibility
issues.body:
## Outcome
Ship a JS SDK that is a true drop-in Sentry replacement for Hyperpush's first canonical integration surface.

## Scope
- JS/TS SDK compatibility and migration path
- "swap DNS and import" level adoption experience
- Ingestion contract, metadata, grouping inputs, releases/envs/session context, and other parity-critical SDK behavior
- Public migration docs for the canonical JS path

## Acceptance criteria
- A real JS app can switch from Sentry to Hyperpush with minimal/no architecture changes
- The Hyperpush JS SDK captures the parity-critical signals the product depends on
- Migration friction is low enough to call it a drop-in replacement truthfully

## Roadmap notes
- Domain: Hyperpush
- Track: Core Parity
- Commitment: Committed
- Critical path: 2

<!-- roadmap-detail-start -->
## Detailed acceptance criteria
- Canonical JS/TS Sentry usage can migrate with import/DNS-level friction rather than a rewrite.
- The JS SDK captures the release, environment, user, session, and stack/context signals the operator app needs.
- The drop-in claim is backed by migration docs and verification fixtures.

## Initial sub-issues
- [ ] https://github.com/hyperpush-org/hyperpush/issues/25 — Hyperpush JS SDK: implement drop-in Sentry compatibility for canonical web/server usage
- [ ] https://github.com/hyperpush-org/hyperpush/issues/26 — Hyperpush JS SDK: ship migration guide and parity verification fixtures
<!-- roadmap-detail-end -->

## Detailed acceptance criteria

issues.title: Hyperpush: core parity for ingestion, grouping, regression, alerts, ownership, and environments
issues.body:
## Outcome
Reach Sentry-parity for the core issue-tracking experience: ingestion, grouping, regression detection, ownership/workflow, environments, alerts, and operator truth.

## Scope
- Ingestion pipeline and project/event model
- Issue grouping and regression detection
- Environments and ownership/workflow state
- Alerts and alert routing
- User/session context and issue detail truth

## Acceptance criteria
- The core operator loop works without hand-waving: issue appears, groups correctly, regresses correctly, and can be acted on
- Public parity claims for the issue workflow are truthful
- Missing parity surfaces are either shipped or removed from claims

## Roadmap notes
- Domain: Hyperpush
- Track: Core Parity
- Commitment: Committed
- Critical path: 2

<!-- roadmap-detail-start -->
## Detailed acceptance criteria
- Production ingestion and the project/event truth model are stable enough for launch.
- Grouping, regression, ownership, environments, alerts, and session context are operator-usable.
- The core incident loop works end to end without hand-waving.

## Initial sub-issues
- [ ] https://github.com/hyperpush-org/hyperpush/issues/29 — Hyperpush core parity: build production ingestion and project/event truth model
- [ ] https://github.com/hyperpush-org/hyperpush/issues/30 — Hyperpush core parity: ship grouping, regression, alerts, ownership, environments, and session context
<!-- roadmap-detail-end -->

issues.title: Hyperpush: parity for releases, deploy correlation, symbolication, performance tracing, replay, retention, and sampling
issues.body:
## Outcome
Reach Sentry-parity for the extended operator surfaces: releases, deploy correlation, source context/symbolication, performance tracing, session replay, retention, and sampling.

## Scope
- Release tracking and deploy correlation
- Source maps / symbolication / source context
- Performance tracing / transaction views
- Session replay
- Retention, quotas, and sampling controls

## Acceptance criteria
- These surfaces are real enough that "1:1 or better than Sentry" is not marketing fiction
- The operator app can inspect and act on these signals as first-class product features
- Any parity gaps are explicit and visible on the roadmap, not hidden in copy

## Roadmap notes
- Domain: Hyperpush
- Track: Core Parity
- Commitment: Committed
- Critical path: 2

<!-- roadmap-detail-start -->
## Detailed acceptance criteria
- Release and deploy correlation plus symbolication/source context are real.
- Performance tracing and session replay are real product surfaces.
- Retention, quotas, and sampling controls are available and operator-meaningful.

## Initial sub-issues
- [ ] https://github.com/hyperpush-org/hyperpush/issues/31 — Hyperpush parity: release/deploy correlation and symbolication/source context
- [ ] https://github.com/hyperpush-org/hyperpush/issues/32 — Hyperpush parity: performance tracing, session replay, retention, and sampling controls
<!-- roadmap-detail-end -->

issues.title: Hyperpush: promote frontend-exp into the real operator app while demoting landing to marketing
issues.body:
## Outcome
Promote `frontend-exp` into the real Hyperpush operator app and reduce `landing` to a pure marketing site.

## Scope
- Rename/reposition `frontend-exp` into the real app surface
- `landing` becomes marketing only
- Replace mocked dashboard surfaces with real operator data and workflows
- All major product screens in the current prototype become truthful: Issues, Performance, Solana Programs, Releases, Alerts, AI Copilot, Bounties, Dashboards, Treasury, Discover

## Acceptance criteria
- The operator app is the real product control plane
- The landing site no longer acts as a fake or partial app
- Core app navigation and page surfaces are backed by real data and workflows

## Roadmap notes
- Domain: Hyperpush
- Track: Operator App
- Commitment: Committed
- Critical path: 3

<!-- roadmap-detail-start -->
## Detailed acceptance criteria
- `frontend-exp` is promoted into the real operator app and `landing` is marketing only.
- The operator app's major screens are backed by real data and workflows.
- The public product surface no longer relies on mocked app behavior.

## Initial sub-issues
- [ ] https://github.com/hyperpush-org/hyperpush/issues/33 — Hyperpush app: promote frontend-exp and retire landing-as-app behavior
- [ ] https://github.com/hyperpush-org/hyperpush/issues/34 — Hyperpush app: replace mocked operator screens with real data and workflows
<!-- roadmap-detail-end -->

issues.title: Hyperpush: SaaS onboarding and control plane at app.hyperpush.dev
issues.body:
## Outcome
Ship the SaaS control plane at `app.hyperpush.dev` for project creation, GitHub connection, AI policy, treasury setup, and overall operator onboarding.

## Scope
- SaaS project creation and configuration
- GitHub connection/setup per project
- AI setup/policy configuration
- Treasury/project token setup for SaaS projects
- Any operator-facing settings required to make the rest of the roadmap usable

## Acceptance criteria
- A new managed project can be created and configured through the app
- SaaS users have a real control plane for GitHub, AI, and treasury setup
- This path is not blocked on hidden manual setup outside the product

## Roadmap notes
- Domain: Hyperpush
- Track: SaaS Growth
- Delivery Mode: SaaS-only
- Commitment: Planned
- Critical path: 3

<!-- roadmap-detail-start -->
## Detailed acceptance criteria
- SaaS users can create a project, connect GitHub, and configure AI policy in product.
- Treasury or token onboarding works at `app.hyperpush.dev`.
- The managed control plane is coherent end to end.

## Initial sub-issues
- [ ] https://github.com/hyperpush-org/hyperpush/issues/35 — Hyperpush SaaS: project creation, GitHub connection, and AI policy setup
- [ ] https://github.com/hyperpush-org/hyperpush/issues/36 — Hyperpush SaaS: treasury/token onboarding and managed operator setup at app.hyperpush.dev
<!-- roadmap-detail-end -->

issues.title: Hyperpush: AI Copilot chat-first operator control for all non-destructive actions
issues.body:
## Outcome
Make the AI Copilot a chat-first operator surface that can do virtually everything non-destructive from the app.

## Scope
- Chat-first operator control for investigation and operational actions
- Non-destructive admin workflows from chat
- Root-cause analysis, summarization, suggested fixes, and broader operator assistance
- By-configured AI for self-hosted via BYO model/provider secrets

## Acceptance criteria
- Operators can manage the platform from chat alone for non-destructive actions
- AI actions match configured project policy rather than hidden product assumptions
- The app/UI and chat surface agree on the same underlying capabilities

## Roadmap notes
- Domain: Hyperpush
- Track: AI + GitHub
- Commitment: Committed
- Critical path: 4

<!-- roadmap-detail-start -->
## Detailed acceptance criteria
- Chat is a first-class operator interface for non-destructive actions.
- AI root-cause analysis, summaries, and suggested actions are real.
- Self-hosted BYO model/provider configuration and policy controls exist.

## Initial sub-issues
- [ ] https://github.com/hyperpush-org/hyperpush/issues/37 — Hyperpush AI Copilot: chat-first root cause analysis and operator actions
- [ ] https://github.com/hyperpush-org/hyperpush/issues/38 — Hyperpush AI Copilot: self-hosted BYO model-provider configuration and policy enforcement
<!-- roadmap-detail-end -->

issues.title: Hyperpush: GitHub autonomous remediation loop (issue → patch → PR → CI → merge)
issues.body:
## Outcome
Close the autonomous engineering loop from production incident to merged fix PR.

## Scope
- Detect/group issue
- Open/update GitHub issue
- Investigate root cause
- Write patch, create branch, push commits
- Open PR, run tests/CI, merge after checks pass
- Respect configured project autonomy rules

## Acceptance criteria
- The agent can autonomously perform the engineering loop when configured
- GitHub is deeply integrated per project/repo, not bolted on as a manual export
- Maintainers can understand and audit what the agent did and why

## Roadmap notes
- Domain: Hyperpush
- Track: AI + GitHub
- Commitment: Committed
- Critical path: 4

<!-- roadmap-detail-start -->
## Detailed acceptance criteria
- The agent can autonomously move from issue creation to patch PR under project policy.
- GitHub installs, permissions, CI, and merge policy are integrated cleanly.
- Maintainers can audit and trust the remediation loop.

## Initial sub-issues
- [ ] https://github.com/hyperpush-org/hyperpush/issues/39 — Hyperpush GitHub loop: automate issue-to-branch-to-PR with audit trail
- [ ] https://github.com/hyperpush-org/hyperpush/issues/40 — Hyperpush GitHub loop: enforce CI, merge policy, and repo permission model
<!-- roadmap-detail-end -->

issues.title: Hyperpush: auto-deploy, post-release verification, and automatic regression reopening
issues.body:
## Outcome
Extend the autonomous loop beyond merge into deployment, post-release verification, and automatic regression reopening.

## Scope
- Deploy automatically if configured
- Use real post-release signals to verify the original error dropped if configured
- Reopen/regress issues automatically when the fix does not hold if configured
- Align this with the first truthful auto-deploy target: generic Docker-on-a-VM

## Acceptance criteria
- Hyperpush can own the post-merge loop on the configured deployment target
- Post-release verification is based on real product/runtime data
- Failed fixes are reopened/regressed automatically when policy allows

## Roadmap notes
- Domain: Hyperpush
- Track: AI + GitHub
- Commitment: Planned
- Critical path: 4 -> 7

<!-- roadmap-detail-start -->
## Detailed acceptance criteria
- Generic Docker-on-a-VM is a truthful automatic deploy target.
- Post-release verification checks whether the original error actually dropped.
- Failed fixes reopen or regress automatically when configured.

## Initial sub-issues
- [ ] https://github.com/hyperpush-org/hyperpush/issues/41 — Hyperpush deploy loop: integrate generic Docker VM auto-deploy
- [ ] https://github.com/hyperpush-org/hyperpush/issues/42 — Hyperpush deploy loop: verify post-release fixes and automatically regress failed ones
<!-- roadmap-detail-end -->

issues.title: Hyperpush: public bug board, contributor race model, maintainer-approved payouts, and X bot distribution
issues.body:
## Outcome
Ship the external contributor loop: public bug board, contributor race model, maintainer-approved payouts, and SaaS-side X announcements.

## Scope
- Private issue elevation to public bug board
- Configurable auto-publication policy
- Contributor flow with wallet address in PR and no mandatory Hyperpush account
- Claim windows where configured; otherwise merged PR wins
- Maintainer-approved payout eligibility after merge to default branch
- Hyperpush X bot announcement for SaaS-side bounties

## Acceptance criteria
- A maintainer can elevate a bug publicly, set or accept a suggested bounty, and have outside contributors compete to fix it
- The merged PR + maintainer approval flow is sufficient to make payout eligible
- The SaaS-side X announcement path is real for public bounties

## Roadmap notes
- Domain: Hyperpush
- Track: Bug Market
- Commitment: Committed
- Critical path: 5

<!-- roadmap-detail-start -->
## Detailed acceptance criteria
- Maintainers can elevate private incidents to a public bug board under policy control.
- Contributors can race on fixes with wallet-in-PR flow and maintainer-approved payout eligibility.
- SaaS-side X distribution for public bounties is real.

## Initial sub-issues
- [ ] https://github.com/hyperpush-org/hyperpush/issues/43 — Hyperpush bug market: public bug board publication and maintainer controls
- [ ] https://github.com/hyperpush-org/hyperpush/issues/44 — Hyperpush bug market: contributor wallet-in-PR flow, payout approval, and X announcements
<!-- roadmap-detail-end -->

issues.title: Hyperpush: Solana treasury, configurable token/SOL payouts, and whale-proof token mechanics
issues.body:
## Outcome
Ship the Solana-side economics: per-project treasury, configurable token/SOL payouts, deployed program, and whale-proof token mechanics by design.

## Scope
- Per-project treasury
- Configurable payout asset: project token or SOL
- Deployed Solana program and payout path
- Hard 3% cap enforced on-chain per wallet
- Sensible exempt wallets (treasury, dev/maintainer, escrow, LP, vesting, and other required system wallets)
- Anti-whale / anti-snipe design chosen truthfully
- Monitoring/detection for suspicious aggregation where hard guarantees are not possible

## Acceptance criteria
- The token/program/payout system is real and can be used by the product
- The maintainer is not required to buy a large defensive position to avoid capture
- Public claims about whale resistance match what is actually enforceable

## Roadmap notes
- Domain: Hyperpush
- Track: Solana Economy
- Commitment: Committed
- Critical path: 6

<!-- roadmap-detail-start -->
## Detailed acceptance criteria
- Program, treasury, and payout path are real with configurable token-or-SOL payouts.
- The 3% per-wallet cap is enforced on-chain with an explicit exempt wallet model.
- Anti-whale launch mechanics and suspicious aggregation monitoring are truthful, not magical.

## Initial sub-issues
- [ ] https://github.com/hyperpush-org/hyperpush/issues/45 — Hyperpush Solana economy: program, treasury, and payout architecture with token-or-SOL configuration
- [ ] https://github.com/hyperpush-org/hyperpush/issues/46 — Hyperpush Solana economy: on-chain whale protection and exempt wallet model
<!-- roadmap-detail-end -->

issues.title: Hyperpush: self-hosted parity with SaaS for all core features using BYO infrastructure and secrets
issues.body:
## Outcome
Self-hosted Hyperpush must have feature parity with SaaS for core product capabilities, using BYO infrastructure and secrets where needed.

## Scope
- Self-hosted parity for core product capabilities
- BYO AI/model provider secrets (for example OpenRouter)
- OSS/manual equivalents for SaaS-specific conveniences where automatic platform features do not exist
- No artificial cloud-only feature wall for the core Hyperpush experience

## Acceptance criteria
- Self-hosted operators can run the full core Hyperpush loop without SaaS lock-in
- The only differences between SaaS and self-hosted are operational convenience and managed infra/secrets
- Public self-hosted claims are truthful and documented

## Roadmap notes
- Domain: Hyperpush
- Track: Deployment
- Delivery Mode: Self-hosted
- Commitment: Committed
- Critical path: 7

<!-- roadmap-detail-start -->
## Detailed acceptance criteria
- Self-hosted users get the full core feature set with BYO infrastructure and secrets where required.
- AI, GitHub, and bug-market parity gaps are closed or explicitly documented.
- Docs and runbooks make the self-hosted contract usable.

## Initial sub-issues
- [ ] https://github.com/hyperpush-org/hyperpush/issues/47 — Hyperpush self-hosted: BYO dependency and deployment contract for full feature parity
- [ ] https://github.com/hyperpush-org/hyperpush/issues/48 — Hyperpush self-hosted: docs, runbooks, and parity closure across AI, GitHub, and bug market
<!-- roadmap-detail-end -->

issues.title: Hyperpush: generic Docker-on-a-VM deployment baseline for frontend and meshable backend
issues.body:
## Outcome
Make Hyperpush easy to deploy anywhere with Docker on a generic VM, with a frontend container and a meshable backend cluster that can auto-mesh.

## Scope
- Generic Docker-on-a-VM deployment target for the product
- Frontend container + meshable backend deployment model
- Runtime/bootstrap expectations for cluster formation
- The first truthful automatic deploy target for Hyperpush

## Acceptance criteria
- A real Hyperpush deployment works on a generic VM with Docker
- The product deploy model is documented and repeatable
- Automatic deployment can truthfully target this platform when configured

## Roadmap notes
- Domain: Hyperpush
- Track: Deployment
- Commitment: Committed
- Critical path: 1 + 7

<!-- roadmap-detail-start -->
## Detailed acceptance criteria
- Product topology is explicit: frontend container plus meshable backend.
- The generic Docker VM reference deployment works.
- Cluster health and bootstrap verification exist for the product deployment path.

## Initial sub-issues
- [ ] https://github.com/hyperpush-org/hyperpush/issues/49 — Hyperpush deployment: define frontend plus meshable backend topology
- [ ] https://github.com/hyperpush-org/hyperpush/issues/50 — Hyperpush deployment: ship generic Docker VM reference deployment and cluster health verification
<!-- roadmap-detail-end -->

issues.title: Hyperpush Launch Roadmap (cross-repo umbrella)
issues.body:
## Scope
This umbrella tracks the public launch roadmap across both repos:
- `hyperpush-org/hyperpush` (product)
- `hyperpush-org/mesh-lang` (language/runtime/docs work required to make Hyperpush truthful)

This roadmap intentionally includes Mesh work when it is on the Hyperpush critical path.

## Critical path
1. Mesh clustered Docker-on-VM deploy baseline for Hyperpush
2. Mesh runtime hardening for Hyperpush production load and clustering
3. Hyperpush JS SDK true drop-in Sentry compatibility
4. Hyperpush Go SDK true drop-in Sentry compatibility
5. Hyperpush core parity for ingestion, grouping, regression, alerts, ownership, and environments
6. Hyperpush parity for releases, deploy correlation, symbolication, performance tracing, replay, retention, and sampling
7. Promote `frontend-exp` into the real operator app while demoting `landing` to marketing
8. Hyperpush AI Copilot chat-first operator control for all non-destructive actions
9. Hyperpush GitHub autonomous remediation loop (issue → patch → PR → CI → merge)
10. Hyperpush public bug board, contributor race model, maintainer-approved payouts, and X bot distribution
11. Hyperpush Solana treasury, configurable token/SOL payouts, and whale-proof token mechanics
12. Hyperpush self-hosted parity with SaaS for all core features using BYO infrastructure and secrets
13. Hyperpush generic Docker-on-a-VM deployment baseline for frontend and meshable backend
14. Mesh deploy and release primitives needed for Hyperpush autonomous remediation
15. Mesh docs, packages, installers, and public surfaces must stay truthful for Hyperpush launch
16. Hyperpush SaaS onboarding and control plane at `app.hyperpush.dev`
17. Hyperpush auto-deploy, post-release verification, and automatic regression reopening

## Mesh foundation epics
- [ ] hyperpush-org/mesh-lang#15 — Mesh: built-in runtime observability canvas for nodes, actors, and message flow
- [ ] hyperpush-org/mesh-lang#3 — Mesh: clustered Docker-on-VM deploy baseline for Hyperpush
- [ ] hyperpush-org/mesh-lang#4 — Mesh: runtime hardening for Hyperpush production load and clustering
- [ ] hyperpush-org/mesh-lang#5 — Mesh: deploy and release primitives needed for Hyperpush autonomous remediation
- [ ] hyperpush-org/mesh-lang#6 — Mesh: docs, packages, installers, and public surfaces must stay truthful for Hyperpush launch

## Hyperpush product epics
- [ ] hyperpush-org/hyperpush#11 — Hyperpush: JS SDK true drop-in Sentry compatibility
- [ ] hyperpush-org/hyperpush#12 — Hyperpush: Go SDK true drop-in Sentry compatibility
- [ ] hyperpush-org/hyperpush#13 — Hyperpush: core parity for ingestion, grouping, regression, alerts, ownership, and environments
- [ ] hyperpush-org/hyperpush#14 — Hyperpush: parity for releases, deploy correlation, symbolication, performance tracing, replay, retention, and sampling
- [ ] hyperpush-org/hyperpush#15 — Hyperpush: promote frontend-exp into the real operator app while demoting landing to marketing
- [ ] hyperpush-org/hyperpush#16 — Hyperpush: SaaS onboarding and control plane at app.hyperpush.dev
- [ ] hyperpush-org/hyperpush#17 — Hyperpush: AI Copilot chat-first operator control for all non-destructive actions
- [ ] hyperpush-org/hyperpush#18 — Hyperpush: GitHub autonomous remediation loop (issue → patch → PR → CI → merge)
- [ ] hyperpush-org/hyperpush#19 — Hyperpush: auto-deploy, post-release verification, and automatic regression reopening
- [ ] hyperpush-org/hyperpush#20 — Hyperpush: public bug board, contributor race model, maintainer-approved payouts, and X bot distribution
- [ ] hyperpush-org/hyperpush#21 — Hyperpush: Solana treasury, configurable token/SOL payouts, and whale-proof token mechanics
- [ ] hyperpush-org/hyperpush#22 — Hyperpush: self-hosted parity with SaaS for all core features using BYO infrastructure and secrets
- [ ] hyperpush-org/hyperpush#23 — Hyperpush: generic Docker-on-a-VM deployment baseline for frontend and meshable backend

## Notes
- `landing` becomes marketing only.
- `frontend-exp` becomes the real operator app (likely renamed/promoted).
- Self-hosted parity is a product requirement, not a separate reduced feature tier.
- SaaS-specific conveniences (for example the Hyperpush X bot) still belong on the roadmap, but not as the core feature boundary.

<!-- roadmap-detail-start -->
## Detailed acceptance criteria
- This umbrella remains the single public cross-repo entry point for the launch plan.
- Every launch-critical epic across Hyperpush and Mesh is attached beneath it.
- The roadmap stays honest about critical path, commitment, and delivery mode.

## Initial sub-issues
- [ ] https://github.com/hyperpush-org/mesh-lang/issues/3 — Mesh: clustered Docker-on-VM deploy baseline for Hyperpush
- [ ] https://github.com/hyperpush-org/mesh-lang/issues/4 — Mesh: runtime hardening for Hyperpush production load and clustering
- [ ] https://github.com/hyperpush-org/mesh-lang/issues/5 — Mesh: deploy and release primitives needed for Hyperpush autonomous remediation
- [ ] https://github.com/hyperpush-org/mesh-lang/issues/6 — Mesh: docs, packages, installers, and public surfaces must stay truthful for Hyperpush launch
- [ ] https://github.com/hyperpush-org/hyperpush/issues/11 — Hyperpush: JS SDK true drop-in Sentry compatibility
- [ ] https://github.com/hyperpush-org/hyperpush/issues/12 — Hyperpush: Go SDK true drop-in Sentry compatibility
- [ ] https://github.com/hyperpush-org/hyperpush/issues/13 — Hyperpush: core parity for ingestion, grouping, regression, alerts, ownership, and environments
- [ ] https://github.com/hyperpush-org/hyperpush/issues/14 — Hyperpush: parity for releases, deploy correlation, symbolication, performance tracing, replay, retention, and sampling
- [ ] https://github.com/hyperpush-org/hyperpush/issues/15 — Hyperpush: promote frontend-exp into the real operator app while demoting landing to marketing
- [ ] https://github.com/hyperpush-org/hyperpush/issues/16 — Hyperpush: SaaS onboarding and control plane at app.hyperpush.dev
- [ ] https://github.com/hyperpush-org/hyperpush/issues/17 — Hyperpush: AI Copilot chat-first operator control for all non-destructive actions
- [ ] https://github.com/hyperpush-org/hyperpush/issues/18 — Hyperpush: GitHub autonomous remediation loop (issue → patch → PR → CI → merge)
- [ ] https://github.com/hyperpush-org/hyperpush/issues/19 — Hyperpush: auto-deploy, post-release verification, and automatic regression reopening
- [ ] https://github.com/hyperpush-org/hyperpush/issues/20 — Hyperpush: public bug board, contributor race model, maintainer-approved payouts, and X bot distribution
- [ ] https://github.com/hyperpush-org/hyperpush/issues/21 — Hyperpush: Solana treasury, configurable token/SOL payouts, and whale-proof token mechanics
- [ ] https://github.com/hyperpush-org/hyperpush/issues/22 — Hyperpush: self-hosted parity with SaaS for all core features using BYO infrastructure and secrets
- [ ] https://github.com/hyperpush-org/hyperpush/issues/23 — Hyperpush: generic Docker-on-a-VM deployment baseline for frontend and meshable backend
<!-- roadmap-detail-end -->

issues.title: Hyperpush JS SDK: implement drop-in Sentry compatibility for canonical web/server usage
issues.body:
## Parent epic
- hyperpush-org/hyperpush#11

## Outcome
Build the JS/TS compatibility layer needed for true drop-in adoption on the first canonical integration surface.

## Acceptance criteria
- Canonical Sentry install path maps to Hyperpush with minimal changes
- Ingestion and metadata parity are real
- Operator app receives the required parity signals

issues.title: Hyperpush JS SDK: ship migration guide and parity verification fixtures
issues.body:
## Parent epic
- hyperpush-org/hyperpush#11

## Outcome
Prove the JS drop-in claim with migration docs and verification fixtures instead of just saying it works.

## Acceptance criteria
- Migration guide exists
- Verification fixtures exercise the parity path
- Public drop-in claim is evidence-backed

issues.title: Hyperpush Go SDK: implement drop-in Sentry compatibility for canonical service usage

- hyperpush-org/hyperpush#12

Build the Go compatibility layer needed for true drop-in adoption on the second canonical integration surface.

- Canonical Go install path maps to Hyperpush with minimal changes
- Ingestion and metadata parity are real
- Operator app receives the required parity signals

issues.title: Hyperpush Go SDK: ship migration guide and parity verification fixtures
issues.body:
## Parent epic
- hyperpush-org/hyperpush#12

Prove the Go drop-in claim with migration docs and verification fixtures instead of relying on copy.

## Acceptance criteria
- Migration guide exists
- Verification fixtures exercise the parity path

issues.title: Hyperpush core parity: audit and harden existing Mesher ingestion and project/event model
issues.body:
## Parent issue
- hyperpush-org/hyperpush#13

## Outcome
Audit and harden the existing Mesher ingestion and project/event model so the operator app and future SDK parity work build on something stable and truthful.

## Current state
Mesher already has a real ingestion path, project/event model, seeded project data, and REST surfaces. This issue is not greenfield buildout; it is about hardening and closing the gaps that would make product claims fake.

## Acceptance criteria
- The current Mesher ingestion and project/event model are audited against the operator app and SDK needs
- Gaps in the current shape are identified and closed or explicitly deferred
- The model is stable enough to support the next wave of app integration work

issues.title: Hyperpush core parity: close remaining gaps in grouping, regression, alerts, ownership, environments, and session context
issues.body:
## Parent issue
- hyperpush-org/hyperpush#13

## Outcome
Close the remaining gaps in grouping, regression detection, alerts, ownership, environments, and session context on top of the current Mesher backend.

## Current state
Mesher already exposes issue search, event detail, dashboard, settings, team, API-key, and alert-rule surfaces. This issue focuses on the product gaps that remain between that backend and the launch-grade operator experience.

## Acceptance criteria
- Grouping and regression behavior are trustworthy
- Alerts, ownership, environments, and session context are operator-usable
- The operator loop no longer relies on copy that overclaims these capabilities

issues.title: Hyperpush parity: release/deploy correlation and symbolication/source context
issues.body:
## Parent epic
- hyperpush-org/hyperpush#14

## Outcome
Make deploys and stack traces investigable enough to meet the extended Sentry-parity bar.

## Acceptance criteria
- Release/deploy correlation is real
- Symbolication/source context is real
- Operators can tie failures to real releases and code locations

issues.title: Hyperpush parity: performance tracing, session replay, retention, and sampling controls
issues.body:
## Parent epic
- hyperpush-org/hyperpush#14

## Outcome
Ship the extended performance and replay surfaces, plus retention/sampling controls, required for launch honesty.

## Acceptance criteria
- Performance tracing is real
- Session replay is real
- Retention and sampling controls exist and are operator-meaningful

issues.title: Hyperpush app: promote frontend-exp and retire landing-as-app behavior
issues.body:
## Parent epic
- hyperpush-org/hyperpush#15

## Outcome
Make `frontend-exp` the real operator app and reduce `landing` to a pure marketing role.

## Acceptance criteria
- App and marketing responsibilities are split cleanly
- frontend-exp is promoted/renamed into the real app surface
- landing no longer acts as a fake app

issues.title: Hyperpush app: replace mocked operator screens with real data and workflows
issues.body:
## Parent epic
- hyperpush-org/hyperpush#15

## Outcome
Replace prototype/mocked screens with real product data and workflows across the operator app.

## Acceptance criteria
- Issues, performance, releases, bounties, treasury, and related screens are real
- Operators can complete meaningful workflows in-app
- UI no longer overclaims unsupported behavior

## Detailed acceptance criteria
- The promoted operator app must stop depending on mock data for the backend surfaces Mesher already exposes today.
- Existing Mesher APIs (issues, dashboard, detail, alerts, settings, team, API keys, storage, and live updates) should be integrated before later-stage Solana/bounty/treasury/discover work is treated as active UI conversion.
- Auth/session/project-selection should be defined as a bridge from the current Mesher contract, not invented independently of it.

## Initial sub-issues
- [ ] https://github.com/hyperpush-org/hyperpush/issues/51 — Hyperpush app backend: define the bridge from the current Mesher contract to real app auth, session, and project selection
- [ ] https://github.com/hyperpush-org/hyperpush/issues/52 — Hyperpush app backend: replace issue, dashboard, detail, and alert mock data with existing Mesher APIs and live updates
- [ ] https://github.com/hyperpush-org/hyperpush/issues/53 — Hyperpush app backend: stage Solana, bounties, treasury, and discover integration behind real backing services
- [ ] https://github.com/hyperpush-org/hyperpush/issues/57 — Hyperpush app backend: wire project settings, team, API keys, and storage admin surfaces to existing Mesher APIs

issues.title: Hyperpush app backend: define the bridge from the current Mesher contract to real app auth, session, and project selection
issues.body:
## Parent issue
- hyperpush-org/hyperpush#34

## Outcome
Define the bridge from the current Mesher project-scoped backend contract to the real multi-project operator app: auth, session, project selection, and the operator-facing API boundary.

## Current state
Mesher already exposes project-scoped REST and WebSocket APIs plus seeded default org/project data, but it does not yet define the full operator auth/session/project-selection model the promoted app will need.

## Acceptance criteria
- The bridge from current Mesher backend assumptions to the real operator app contract is explicit
- Auth/session/project-selection expectations are defined truthfully
- The app/backend boundary is stable enough that the rest of the app integration work is not guessing

issues.title: Hyperpush app backend: replace issue, dashboard, detail, and alert mock data with existing Mesher APIs and live updates
issues.body:
## Parent issue
- hyperpush-org/hyperpush#34

## Outcome
Replace the mocked issue, dashboard, event-detail, and alert surfaces in `frontend-exp` using the Mesher APIs and live-update path that already exist.

## Current state
Mesher already exposes project-scoped REST and WebSocket surfaces for:
- issues / search / event detail
- dashboard metrics and timelines
- alert rules and alert state
- live event and issue updates

This issue is about wiring those real backend surfaces into the app instead of treating the whole operator product as a mock.

## Acceptance criteria
- Issues list and issue detail use live Mesher data
- Dashboard metrics/charts use live Mesher data where the backend already exists
- Alert surfaces use live Mesher data where the backend already exists
- The app uses real-time updates where Mesher already broadcasts them

issues.title: Hyperpush app backend: stage Solana, bounties, treasury, and discover integration behind real backing services
issues.body:
## Parent issue
- hyperpush-org/hyperpush#34

## Outcome
Keep the Solana, bounties, treasury, and discover app integration work explicitly staged behind the backing services it depends on.

## Current state
Those screens exist in `frontend-exp`, but the backend/service layer for them is not yet equivalent to the current Mesher issue/dashboard surfaces. This issue is not the first integration step; it is the later app-side integration once those backing services become real.

## Acceptance criteria
- The integration contract for Solana, bounties, treasury, and discover is explicit
- The issue is sequenced after the backing services exist, not treated as immediate UI wiring
- The roadmap stays honest about what is blocked on backend/service work

issues.title: Hyperpush deploy topology: split landing marketing from frontend-exp app routing and runtime boundaries

## Parent issue
- hyperpush-org/hyperpush#49

## Outcome
Define the concrete runtime boundary between `landing` as marketing and `frontend-exp` as the real operator app.

## Acceptance criteria
- Marketing vs app hostnames/routes are explicit
- Frontend and backend runtime responsibilities are explicit
- The topology is concrete enough to implement in Docker on a generic VM

issues.title: Hyperpush deployment: add a production Dockerfile and container startup path for frontend-exp
issues.body:
## Parent issue
- hyperpush-org/hyperpush#50

## Outcome
Give `frontend-exp` a real production container path instead of relying on local-only dev assumptions.

## Acceptance criteria
- `frontend-exp` has a production Dockerfile or equivalent container build path
- Container startup contract is explicit
- The app can run as a real service in the generic VM deployment model

issues.title: Hyperpush deployment: create generic-VM compose stack and health verification for landing + frontend-exp + mesher backend
issues.body:
## Parent issue
- hyperpush-org/hyperpush#50

Create the reference generic-VM deployment stack and a health verification flow for the marketing site, app, and backend together.

## Acceptance criteria
- A repeatable generic-VM stack exists
- Health/readiness verification exists for the deployed surfaces
- The stack reflects the real product topology rather than a mock/demo shortcut

issues.title: Hyperpush app backend: wire project settings, team, API keys, and storage admin surfaces to existing Mesher APIs
issues.body:
## Parent issue
- hyperpush-org/hyperpush#34

## Outcome
Bridge the existing Mesher admin surfaces into the operator app: project settings, team/member management, API keys, and storage visibility.

## Current state
Mesher already exposes project/org admin APIs for:
- project settings
- storage visibility
- team/member management
- API key creation and revocation

This is upstream app-integration work that can proceed before the broader Solana/bounty/treasury screens are real.

## Acceptance criteria
- The operator app can use the existing settings, team, API-key, and storage APIs
- The current backend contract is reflected in the app honestly
- These admin surfaces no longer depend on future-only product capabilities
